mnemonics: UFOs UnFold Over time. Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) are like UnFolding a computational graph Over sequential steps to handle sequences.
keyPoints: Unfolding is the process of replicating the same RNN cell for each time step in a sequence, creating a deep computational graph.
This process allows the network to share parameters (the same weights) across all time steps, making it efficient for sequences of variable length.
Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) is the training algorithm used on this unfolded graph, which can suffer from vanishing or exploding gradients.
commonMistakes: Confusing Recursive Neural Networks (which operate on tree structures) with Recurrent Neural Networks (which operate on sequences).
Assuming the unfolded graph has independent parameters for each step; the parameters are actually shared across the unfolded time steps.
